skill.name: schema-alignment
skill.description: Detect drift between database schemas, migrations, and ORM data models across common Python and TypeScript stacks.
skill.summary: Find schema drift between database structures, migrations, and application models.
skill.author: Consiliency

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, network, environment, and weak-crypto patterns, but manual review found most are Markdown examples or ORM syntax. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, hidden network call, or malicious intent was found. The remaining risk is operational because the skill can guide agents to inspect database metadata and run migration commands that may alter schema state.

medium_findings.title: Database Migration Commands Require Human Approval
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE operational risk. The Alembic cookbook documents revision, upgrade, downgrade, add-column, and drop-column workflows. These are legitimate for schema alignment, but executing them without explicit approval can change or remove database schema state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands and migration examples are clearly present and can affect schema state. Confidence is below very high because they are documentation examples, not automatically executed code.

medium_findings.title: Live Database Introspection May Expose Schema Metadata
medium_findings.description: NEEDS_REVIEW operational risk. The skill allows querying information_schema if accessible and shows a Prisma datasource using DATABASE_URL. This is normal database tooling, but users should avoid exposing credentials or schema metadata outside the project context.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence shows possible database access and a credential-bearing environment variable reference. I did not find network exfiltration or instructions to reveal the connection string.

low_findings.title: External Command Detections Are Mostly Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for code execution. The static analyzer matched fenced Markdown snippets and inline ORM syntax across the skill. The reviewed files do not contain executable scripts, hooks, or dynamic shell invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation blocks rather than runnable skill code. Some commands are still operationally sensitive if a user chooses to run them.

low_findings.title: Network Detection Is A JSON Schema Example
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for network activity. The only reviewed hardcoded URL is an example JSON Schema identifier, not code that sends data over the network.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears inside an output schema example. I found no fetch, curl, webhook, or other network transfer instruction at that location.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for weak cryptography. The cited locations are skill metadata, headings, Markdown tables, migration placeholders, and JSON field names. No hashing, encryption, signing, or random generation logic was found there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain documentation text and examples, not cryptographic APIs. The static labels appear to come from broad keyword matching.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands In Cookbook Snippets
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell snippets for migration status checks, file discovery, and AI-dev-kit invocation. These commands are legitimate but should be treated as user-approved actions, not automatic execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and easy for an agent to follow. They are not hidden code and have a legitimate schema-alignment purpose.

dangerous_patterns.title: Schema-Altering Migration Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The Alembic cookbook demonstrates add, drop, alter, upgrade, and downgrade operations. These examples can guide destructive changes when applied to a real database.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples directly name schema-changing Alembic operations. The risk depends on whether the user applies them to production or a test environment.

content.value_statement: Database models and migrations often drift apart as projects evolve. This skill compares schemas, ORM models, and migrations so teams can find alignment issues before release.
actual_capabilities: Identifies ORM and migration stacks from common project indicators.
Compares database schema details with application model definitions.
Reports missing columns, type mismatches, nullable mismatches, and foreign key drift.
Provides SQLAlchemy, Prisma, and Alembic detection guidance.
Suggests migration or model changes for reviewed alignment issues.
limitations: Live database checks require approved access to the target database.
Migration suggestions require human review before execution.
Custom ORM patterns may need project-specific interpretation.
The skill reports drift but does not guarantee production safety.

use_cases.title: Review Pull Requests With Model Changes
use_cases.description: Check whether changed models have matching migrations and compatible database constraints before merging.

use_cases.title: Audit Legacy Database Projects
use_cases.description: Compare existing migrations, live schema metadata, and ORM models to find drift in older services.

use_cases.title: Prepare Safer Migration Plans
use_cases.description: Identify risky schema differences before migration commands are reviewed, tested, or added to CI.

prompt_templates.title: Check My Schema Alignment
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a project with database models.
prompt_templates.prompt: Detect the database and ORM stack in this project. Compare models, migrations, and schema sources, then summarize any drift.

prompt_templates.title: Review A Pull Request For Drift
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the current changes for schema drift. Focus on model fields, migrations, nullability, defaults, and type constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Prisma Schema Consistency
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the Prisma schema, migrations, and generated model usage. Report mismatches that could cause runtime or migration issues.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Alembic Fixes Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare SQLAlchemy models with Alembic migrations. Propose safe migration steps, rollback concerns, and tests without executing database commands.

output_examples.input: A SQLAlchemy project adds a required model field but has no matching migration.
output_examples.output: The report flags a missing database column, marks it medium or high based on nullability, and recommends a reviewed migration.

output_examples.input: A Prisma schema uses an optional field while application code treats it as required.
output_examples.output: The report identifies a nullable mismatch and explains the possible runtime failure before suggesting an alignment path.

output_examples.input: An Alembic migration changes a string column length.
output_examples.output: The report notes the type constraint change, highlights possible data truncation, and recommends testing with representative data.

best_practices: Run the skill before merging model, migration, or database access changes.
Review generated migration suggestions in a test environment first.
Document intentional drift so later audits do not treat it as an error.
anti_patterns: Running upgrade or downgrade commands without explicit user approval.
Treating live database metadata as safe to expose in shared logs.
Ignoring nullable, default, or length mismatches because tests still pass.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my database automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides analysis and migration guidance. Users should approve and run any database commands themselves.

faq.question: Which ORMs does it support?
faq.answer: It includes guidance for SQLAlchemy, Django ORM, Prisma, TypeORM, Drizzle, Sequelize, Knex, and related migrations.

faq.question: Can it inspect a live database?
faq.answer: It can guide live schema inspection when access is available. Use approved credentials and avoid exposing connection strings.

faq.question: Is it useful without database access?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare migrations and model definitions directly from repository files.

faq.question: Can it generate migration fixes?
faq.answer: It can suggest migration changes. Review, test, and approve every change before applying it.

faq.question: Does it replace human database review?
faq.answer: No. It helps find drift, but schema changes still require engineering review and environment-specific testing.
